postData.contentHtml:
In a major uplift for Irish rugby, winger Rob Baloucoune and prop Jeremy Loughman are back in full training and declared fit to play against New Zealand. Their return comes as a timely boost for the Ireland squad, injecting experience and firepower ahead of what promises to be a challenging encounter with the All Blacks.

While Baloucoune was previously sidelined and missed the game against Australia, recent reports confirm his and Loughman's readiness for the upcoming clash. This news has been met with largely positive sentiment across social media, with fans and pundits eagerly anticipating their impact on the field.

Ireland's preparations to face New Zealand will undoubtedly be strengthened by the availability of these key players, as the team looks to leverage their renewed health and skill against one of rugby's most dominant forces.
postData.title: Ireland Gets Major Boost: Baloucoune and Loughman Fit for All Blacks Clash
postData.summary: Irish rugby stars Rob Baloucoune and Jeremy Loughman have made a full recovery from their injuries and are set to return to action, providing a significant advantage for Ireland as they prepare to face the formidable New Zealand All Blacks.
